meta.title: Pippu Ski Area
meta.description: Dry powder under the Daisetsu range—lift tickets bundled with bath access, away from mega-resort crowds.

hero.eyebrow: Pippu, Kamikawa, Hokkaido · town of ski and strawberries
hero.title:
Pippu
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Dry powder under the Daisetsu range—lift tickets bundled with bath access, away from mega-resort crowds.

sanctuary.title:
Dry cold powder,
empty runs.
sanctuary.lead: A major local hill overlooking the Kamikawa basin. Inland cold down to −12°C at the summit brings silky powder without lift-line battles—the "unspoiled" experience mega resorts can't sell, for a ¥3,800 day pass.
sanctuary.noticeBody: Own domain (pippu.ski) and live cameras for snow and road conditions—rare digital openness for a town-run area. Lift 3 was retired after 2023–24 to cut maintenance and sustain long-term operations.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Kamikawa basin under the Daisetsu range—quiet powder runs

primary.title: Strawberry 2,200 m × expert 36°
primary.lead: 30% beginner, 50% intermediate, 20% advanced. Family 2,000 m runs and the expert "Deep Snow Veteran" at 36° max—separate flow lines so every level skis without getting in each other's way.
primary.imgAlt: Varied courses across the hill—Daisetsu panorama

secondary.title: Three ski schools
secondary.lead: SAJ-certified Pippu Ski School, JSBA snowboard school, and a mogul specialty school (classroom + on-snow). Help groom the course and get a discount—a design that builds ownership of the hill.

bundle.title:
Lift tickets
include the bath.
bundle.lead: Day and night tickets include entry to adjacent Yu-yu Pippu (normally ¥600). Effective lift price ~¥3,200—about one-third of nearby mega resorts. After skiing, bath → dining and local products keep spending inside Pippu town.
bundle.imgAlt: Yu-yu Pippu—Komeiseki bath and lodging beside the slopes

wellness.title:
Then TKG
and karaage.
wellness.lead: Hokurei Lodge in the center house serves legendary karaage bowls and miso ramen beyond typical slope food. Next door, Yu-yu Pippu's Komeiseki bath eases sore legs; morning brings "ultimate TKG"—Kappa eggs, Yukime rice, Pippu scallion soy.
wellness.leadStation: Pipicafe at JR Pippu Station serves TKG (¥520) for the ride home.
wellness.imgAlt: Yu-yu Pippu breakfast buffet—farm-direct egg over rice

tour.title:
Asahikawa hub,
Pippu payoff.
tour.lead: Friday evening in Asahikawa for city food. Saturday bus to Pippu, ski, bundled bath, stay at Yu-yu Pippu, Sunday TKG breakfast—a trip that beats mega-resort price for experience quality.

01.title: Asahikawa hotel
01.body: OMO7 Asahikawa, Dormy Inn—station-front city dining

02.title: Dohoku Bus direct
02.body: Five daily runs from Asahikawa Station · ~1 hour

03.title: Pippu Ski Area
03.body: ¥3,800 day pass · lunch at Hokurei Lodge

05.title: Morning TKG
05.body: Terroir breakfast, then midday flight home

ecosystem.title: Bath bundle & local economy
ecosystem.body: Bundling onsen with lift tickets returns spending to Kappa eggs, scallion soy, and strawberry farms—profit stays local while avoiding overtourism. Season pass ¥45,000; buy one adult season pass and all elementary kids in the household ski free.

access.title: Asahikawa hub & last mile
access.body: Asahikawa Airport to station ~40 min; bus to the area ~1 hour. Free shuttle from JR Pippu Station (3 round trips daily)—hands-free without a rental car. Departures 08:50, 11:00, 13:50 from the station.

green.title: Strawberries in summer, ski in winter
green.body: Mid-June–early July strawberry picking at five farms. Green Park Pippu camping and park golf—winter families get a reason to return in summer.

access.note: Asahikawa Airport → station ~40 min · Dohoku Bus from station ~1 hr (5/day) · free shuttle from JR Pippu · parking available
access.phoneBus: Dohoku Bus · shuttle times on official site
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido上川郡Kamikawa-gun比布町Pippu北7線Kita-7-sen17
access.rentacarEyebrow: 雪・食・温泉の隠れ家
access.rentacarLink: 旭川空港でレンタカー予約
access.rentacarNote: スカイチケット（外部サイト）
access.rentacarHint: ぴっぷスキー場周辺のドライブ向け

hero.title:
Four tastes
beyond slope food.
hero.tagline: From karaage bowls to ultimate TKG—Pippu terroir

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and menus are on each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introLead: Quality beyond typical ski cafeterias builds repeat visits and word of mouth.
nearbyFood.introBody: On the hill, after skiing, and at the station—four picks along your route.
nearbyFood.spotsLead: From the center house into town, in route order.

hokurei.title: Hokurei Lodge (Skiip)
hokurei.lead: A local institution for decades. Juicy karaage bowls, house-miso ramen (¥1,000), and agataiyaki pancakes (¥120)—skier favorites.

yuyu.title: Yu-yu Pippu restaurant
yuyu.lead: Half-board breakfast with unlimited "ultimate TKG"—Kappa eggs, Kamikawa rice, Pippu scallion soy.

pipicafe.title: Pipicafe Pippu Station
pipicafe.lead: TKG on the way home—terroir in the JR Pippu Station building.

otokoyama.title: Otokoyama (Pippu)
otokoyama.lead: Pair skiing with a sake brewery visit—taste Pippu's brewing tradition.

hero.title:
Komeiseki bath,
ease the legs.
hero.tagline: Where bundled bath tickets go—Yu-yu Pippu

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees and hours are on each facility's official site.
nearbyOnsen.introTitle: Onsen at the core of the bundle
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Day and night tickets include bath entry—the après route is designed from the start.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Yu-yu Pippu beside the slopes, plus Asahikawa city baths for hub-stay plans.
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Recovery after skiing and lodging bases.

yuyu.title: Yu-yu Pippu
yuyu.lead: Quasi-drug certified Komeiseki mineral bath. Five-minute walk from slopes; half-board from ¥9,500.

kamoi.title: Kamoi no Yu Dormy Inn Asahikawa
kamoi.lead: Hub-stay bathing—sauna and open-air bath, then bus to Pippu next morning.

hero.title:
Stay in Pippu,
or hub in Asahikawa.

corridorStay.notice: Rooms, rates, and shuttles are on each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Sleep at Yu-yu Pippu for dawn powder, or stay in Asahikawa for city food and bus commute—choose by what your trip is about.

yuyu.title: Yu-yu Pippu
yuyu.lead: Closest to the hill. Maximize the bath bundle and morning TKG—built for first-track skiers.

mount-city.title: Mount City
mount-city.lead: In-town lodging—an alternative to Yu-yu Pippu within Pippu.

omo7.title: Hoshino Resorts OMO7 Asahikawa
omo7.lead: Flagship hub stay—Asahikawa ramen and Genghis Khan at night, Dohoku Bus to Pippu in the morning.

dormy.title: Kamoi no Yu Dormy Inn Asahikawa
dormy.lead: Low friction near the station. Strong choice for bathing before the trip home.
